hypothesis: If metal superhydride discovery is structured around the chemical concept of quasi-atom electron localization in metal lattices as a primary screening criterion combined with machine learning predictions of thermodynamic stability, then this workflow will more efficiently identify novel stable and high-temperature superconducting metal superhydrides compared to unguided high-throughput searches, because constraining the structural search space using chemically meaningful descriptors reduces computational cost while preserving the most promising candidates for DFT validation.
